SEARCH
Package com.orasaka.gateway.service

Class ChatStreamService

java.lang.Object
com.orasaka.gateway.service.ChatStreamService
@Service public class ChatStreamService extends Object
Service orchestrating AI chat streaming flows for SSE and GraphQL subscriptions.

Implements the strict Service-Controller boundary by decoupling controllers from execution thread pools, payload orchestration, and context layout assembly.

All asynchronous streaming flows are initiated and processed utilizing Java 21 Virtual Threads.

See Also:
  • OrasakaAiClient
  • OrasakaContext
  • SseEmitter

  • Constructor Details

    • ChatStreamService

      public ChatStreamService(OrasakaAiClient aiClient)
      Initializes the service with the AI client and a virtual thread executor.
      Parameters:
      aiClient - The core AI client facade.

  • Method Details

    • streamSse

      public void streamSse(String conversationId, String prompt, User user, org.springframework.web.servlet.mvc.method.annotation.SseEmitter emitter)
      Subscribes to the reactive AI token stream and emits Server-Sent Events (SSE).

      Executes asynchronously on a Virtual Thread to avoid blocking the main Servlet engine.

      Parameters:
      conversationId - The session identifier for thread isolation.
      prompt - The raw user message input.
      user - The currently authenticated user.
      emitter - The target SseEmitter connection.

    • streamGraphQL

      public reactor.core.publisher.Flux<OrasakaChatResponse> streamGraphQL(String conversationId, String prompt, User user)
      Resolves the reactive stream context and returns a Flux for GraphQL subscriptions.

      Assembles context dynamically without artificial thread sleeps.

      Parameters:
      conversationId - The session identifier for thread isolation.
      prompt - The raw user message input.
      user - The currently authenticated user.
      Returns:
      A reactive Flux emitting OrasakaChatResponse chunks.
      See Also:
      • OrasakaAiClient.stream(OrasakaChatRequest)
